title: TARGETARCH automatically set in docker but not podman
body:
### Feature request description

When building Dockerfiles like this one, it builds with docker and not podman. docker sets targetarch automatically while podman doesn't:

https://github.com/docker/model-runner/blob/main/Dockerfile

### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: --platform linux/amd64 might make a difference to docker build.

Using `--build-arg TARGETARCH ...` as a workaround, to fulfill `ARG TARGETARCH`

Also BUILDPLATFORM is missing, for lines like `FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM`
@nalind ptal , is this a bug or intentful ?

The docs are subtle on whether or not built-in args need to be declared before they're used in the heading, which is a requirement in regular build stages, i.e. at any point after a FROM instruction.  The example at https://github.com/docker/model-runner/blob/main/Dockerfile suggests that they shouldn't need to be declared before using them in the heading, but our implementation isn't relaxing its requirement for the heading.  That's a bug.  Until we fix that, declaring it before use, like you'd have to do in a particular stage, is harmless, and it appears to be enough to keep the build going.
@nalind should we transfer this to buildah?
Sure.  I expect the bug fix will go either there, in https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder, or in both.

> Could I work on it?

Go for it.

This looks to have been fixed with:  https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/312 which has recently merged.

@nalind or @NotSoFancyName if you disagree, please reopen.  Regardless, @NotSoFancyName TYVM for the imagebuilder PR!
